sections.paragraph:
διακαθαίρω 1 aor. inf. διακαθᾶραι (s. καθαρίζω; Aristoph., Pla. et al.; BCH 27, 1903, 73 ln. 79) to make free of someth. unwanted, thoroughly purge, clean out τὶ a threshingfloor Lk 3:17 (Alciphron 2, 23, 1 ἄρτι μοι τὴν ἅλω διακαθήραντι).—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

BCH BCH = Bulletin de correspondance hellénique—List 6

  1. ln. = line

someth. someth. = something

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 229
